about.overview.title: Company Overview
kr.about.overview.title: Company Overview
about.overview.p1: HicareNet is a digital healthcare company developing a SaaS platform for remote patient management under the US Medicare system.
kr.about.overview.p1: HicareNet is a digital healthcare company developing a SaaS platform for remote patient management under the US Medicare system.
about.overview.p2: Built on over 20 years of remote patient monitoring technology accumulated by the healthcare division of its parent company, InSung Information, HicareNet was established as an independent entity through a spin-off in 2020. In 2021, the company set up a US subsidiary in California and launched Hicare Care Portal, a SaaS platform that supports RPM (Remote Patient Monitoring) and CCM (Chronic Care Management) billing for US clinics.
kr.about.overview.p2: Built on over 20 years of remote patient monitoring technology accumulated by the healthcare division of its parent company, InSung Information, HicareNet was established as an independent entity through a spin-off in 2020. In 2021, the company set up a US subsidiary in California and launched Hicare Care Portal, a SaaS platform that supports RPM (Remote Patient Monitoring) and CCM (Chronic Care Management) billing for US clinics.
about.overview.p3: As of 2025, HicareNet has contracts with 40 US clinics, serves over 3,000 patients, and processes more than 300 insurance claims per month. With overseas revenue accounting for 96% of total revenue, the company operates a US-focused global business structure.
kr.about.overview.p3: As of 2025, HicareNet has contracts with 40 US clinics, serves over 3,000 patients, and processes more than 300 insurance claims per month. With overseas revenue accounting for 96% of total revenue, the company operates a US-focused global business structure.
about.mission.title: Mission
kr.about.mission.title: Mission
about.mission.statement: In the US Medicare chronic care management market, we improve both clinic profitability and patient health outcomes through technology-driven billing automation. Our goal is to create an environment where clinicians can focus on patient care instead of administrative tasks.
kr.about.mission.statement: In the US Medicare chronic care management market, we improve both clinic profitability and patient health outcomes through technology-driven billing automation. Our goal is to create an environment where clinicians can focus on patient care instead of administrative tasks.

business.title: Business
business.hero.headline:
Solving Clinic Billing
Through Technology
business.hero.subtext:
From automated RPM & CCM billing to patient monitoring —
we reduce the operational complexity clinics face every day.
kr.business.title: Business
business.care_portal.title: Hicare Care Portal
kr.business.care_portal.title: Hicare Care Portal
business.care_portal.intro: A SaaS platform that automates RPM/CCM billing for US clinics.
kr.business.care_portal.intro: A SaaS platform that automates RPM/CCM billing for US clinics.
business.medicare.title: Medicare and Remote Patient Management
kr.business.medicare.title: Medicare and Remote Patient Management
business.medicare.p1: Medicare is a US public insurance program for individuals aged 65 and older and certain people with disabilities. Medicare reimburses clinics for remote health management of patients with chronic conditions.
kr.business.medicare.p1: Medicare is a US public insurance program for individuals aged 65 and older and certain people with disabilities. Medicare reimburses clinics for remote health management of patients with chronic conditions.
business.medicare.p2: RPM (Remote Patient Monitoring) is a service that remotely collects and analyzes patient vital data such as blood pressure and blood glucose. CCM (Chronic Care Management) is a non-face-to-face care management service for patients with chronic conditions. Clinics that provide these services can bill Medicare to generate revenue.
kr.business.medicare.p2: RPM (Remote Patient Monitoring) is a service that remotely collects and analyzes patient vital data such as blood pressure and blood glucose. CCM (Chronic Care Management) is a non-face-to-face care management service for patients with chronic conditions. Clinics that provide these services can bill Medicare to generate revenue.
business.medicare.p3: Care Portal is a SaaS platform that automates the entire billing process for these services.
kr.business.medicare.p3: Care Portal is a SaaS platform that automates the entire billing process for these services.
business.care_portal.problem.title: The Problem Clinics Face
kr.business.care_portal.problem.title: The Problem Clinics Face
business.care_portal.problem.description: To bill for RPM/CCM, clinics must document detailed deliverables including patient care records, monitoring data, consultation notes, and management time logs. This administrative burden is significant for clinicians, and incomplete documentation leads to missed claims.
kr.business.care_portal.problem.descript: To bill for RPM/CCM, clinics must document detailed deliverables including patient care records, monitoring data, consultation notes, and management time logs. This administrative burden is significant for clinicians, and incomplete documentation leads to missed claims.
business.care_portal.solution.title: How Care Portal Solves It
kr.business.care_portal.solution.title: How Care Portal Solves It
business.care_portal.problem1.title: Patient Care Workflow
kr.business.care_portal.problem1.title: Patient Care Workflow
business.care_portal.problem1.desc: Patient enrollment, vital data monitoring, abnormal value alerts, consultation logging, and care plan management are all handled within a single platform.
kr.business.care_portal.problem1.desc: Patient enrollment, vital data monitoring, abnormal value alerts, consultation logging, and care plan management are all handled within a single platform.
business.care_portal.problem2.title: Automated Documentation
kr.business.care_portal.problem2.title: Automated Documentation
business.care_portal.problem2.desc: Automatically generates the deliverables required for RPM/CCM billing, including management time, consultation records, and monitoring logs. Reduces clinician administrative burden and prevents missed claims.
kr.business.care_portal.problem2.desc: Automatically generates the deliverables required for RPM/CCM billing, including management time, consultation records, and monitoring logs. Reduces clinician administrative burden and prevents missed claims.
business.care_portal.problem3.title: Billing Integration
kr.business.care_portal.problem3.title: Billing Integration
business.care_portal.problem3.desc: Automatically generates reports aligned with RPM CPT codes (99453, 99454, 99457, 99458) and CCM CPT codes (99490, 99487, 99489), linking them to Medicare insurance claims.
kr.business.care_portal.problem3.desc: Automatically generates reports aligned with RPM CPT codes (99453, 99454, 99457, 99458) and CCM CPT codes (99490, 99487, 99489), linking them to Medicare insurance claims.
business.care_portal.flow.description: HicareNet (provides Care Portal) -> Clinic (manages patients, performs monitoring) -> Patient (transmits vital data) -> CMS (reimburses insurance claims)
kr.business.care_portal.flow.description: HicareNet (provides Care Portal) -> Clinic (manages patients, performs monitoring) -> Patient (transmits vital data) -> CMS (reimburses insurance claims)
business.care_portal.flow.cms_note: CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) is the US public insurance administration agency. The process is: clinics manage patients, document via Care Portal, then submit claims to CMS.
kr.business.care_portal.flow.cms_note: CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) is the US public insurance administration agency. The process is: clinics manage patients, document via Care Portal, then submit claims to CMS.
business.care_portal.flow.device_note: Medical devices are FDA-approved devices owned by the clinics themselves. HicareNet does not supply or integrate devices.
kr.business.care_portal.flow.device_note: Medical devices are FDA-approved devices owned by the clinics themselves. HicareNet does not supply or integrate devices.
business.care_portal.feat1: Clinician Dashboard: Patient list, abnormal value alerts, priority-based sorting
kr.business.care_portal.feat1: Clinician Dashboard: Patient list, abnormal value alerts, priority-based sorting
business.care_portal.feat2: AI-Powered Report Generation: Automatically generates documentation meeting CPT code-specific requirements
kr.business.care_portal.feat2: AI-Powered Report Generation: Automatically generates documentation meeting CPT code-specific requirements
business.care_portal.feat3: Time Tracking & Documentation Automation: Automatically logs call records, care plans, and management time
kr.business.care_portal.feat3: Time Tracking & Documentation Automation: Automatically logs call records, care plans, and management time
business.care_portal.feat4: Multilingual UI: English, Spanish, and Korean
kr.business.care_portal.feat4: Multilingual UI: English, Spanish, and Korean
business.care_portal.feat5: Patient App (Hicare App): Senior-friendly interface with voice-guided measurement prompts
kr.business.care_portal.feat5: Patient App (Hicare App): Senior-friendly interface with voice-guided measurement prompts
business.care_portal.revenue.title: Revenue Model
kr.business.care_portal.revenue.title: Revenue Model
business.care_portal.revenue.description: A SaaS monthly subscription model. Clinics pay HicareNet a platform usage fee. Clinics use Care Portal to manage patients and bill Medicare for reimbursement. Revenue generation of approximately $50 per patient per month is possible (varies by condition and service type).
kr.business.care_portal.revenue.descript: A SaaS monthly subscription model. Clinics pay HicareNet a platform usage fee. Clinics use Care Portal to manage patients and bill Medicare for reimbursement. Revenue generation of approximately $50 per patient per month is possible (varies by condition and service type).

kr.business.care_portal.performance.clai: More than 300 insurance claims processed per month

business.expansion.title: Service Expansion Roadmap
kr.business.expansion.title: Service Expansion Roadmap
business.expansion.intro: Building on Care Portal as the foundation, we are expanding into the following areas.
kr.business.expansion.intro: Building on Care Portal as the foundation, we are expanding into the following areas.
business.expansion.apcm.title: APCM (Advanced Primary Care Management)
kr.business.expansion.apcm.title: APCM (Advanced Primary Care Management)
business.expansion.apcm.description: A new primary care-based chronic care management reimbursement program introduced by CMS in 2025. It extends the existing CCM model with CPT codes G0556, G0557, and G0558. Care Portal will support APCM workflows.
kr.business.expansion.apcm.description: A new primary care-based chronic care management reimbursement program introduced by CMS in 2025. It extends the existing CCM model with CPT codes G0556, G0557, and G0558. Care Portal will support APCM workflows.
business.expansion.awv.title: AWV (Annual Wellness Visit)
kr.business.expansion.awv.title: AWV (Annual Wellness Visit)
business.expansion.awv.description: An annual health checkup program for Medicare beneficiaries. Requires completion of an HRA form and creation of a personalized prevention plan. Care Portal is preparing workflow automation for AWV.
kr.business.expansion.awv.description: An annual health checkup program for Medicare beneficiaries. Requires completion of an HRA form and creation of a personalized prevention plan. Care Portal is preparing workflow automation for AWV.
business.expansion.hra.title: HRA (Health Risk Assessment)
kr.business.expansion.hra.title: HRA (Health Risk Assessment)
business.expansion.hra.description: A required component of AWV for health risk evaluation. We plan to develop form automation and results analysis features.
kr.business.expansion.hra.description: A required component of AWV for health risk evaluation. We plan to develop form automation and results analysis features.
business.expansion.vbc.title: VBC (Value-Based Care)
kr.business.expansion.vbc.title: VBC (Value-Based Care)
business.expansion.vbc.description: The shift from fee-for-service to outcomes-based reimbursement. After accumulating RPM/CCM/APCM data, we plan to evolve into a platform that supports contracts based on patient health outcomes.
kr.business.expansion.vbc.description: The shift from fee-for-service to outcomes-based reimbursement. After accumulating RPM/CCM/APCM data, we plan to evolve into a platform that supports contracts based on patient health outcomes.
business.devices.title: Accumulated Medical Domain Expertise
kr.business.devices.title: Accumulated Medical Domain Expertise
business.devices.intro: HicareNet has accumulated over 20 years of medical device integration technology since its parent company InSung Information era. This hardware and medical domain experience serves as the foundation for the current Care Portal software business.
kr.business.devices.intro: HicareNet has accumulated over 20 years of medical device integration technology since its parent company InSung Information era. This hardware and medical domain experience serves as the foundation for the current Care Portal software business.

kr.business.devices.hub.title: Hicare Hub (Gateway)
business.devices.hub.description: A gateway product compatible with over 120 types of medical devices. It holds medical device certifications and has been commercialized for over 15 years, with exports to international markets.
kr.business.devices.hub.description: A gateway product compatible with over 120 types of medical devices. It holds medical device certifications and has been commercialized for over 15 years, with exports to international markets.

careers.challenge.title: Problems We Are Solving
kr.careers.challenge.title: Problems We Are Solving
careers.challenge.intro: HicareNet's R&D team uses technology to solve real problems in the US healthcare market. These are the challenges our team is currently focused on.
kr.careers.challenge.intro: HicareNet's R&D team uses technology to solve real problems in the US healthcare market. These are the challenges our team is currently focused on.
careers.challenge.1.title: AI-Powered Report Generation
kr.careers.challenge.1.title: AI-Powered Report Generation
careers.challenge.1.description: US Medicare RPM/CCM billing requires deliverables in specific formats for each CPT code. We are developing an AI pipeline that analyzes patient vital data and care records to automatically generate reports meeting each code's requirements. The goal is to reduce clinician administrative burden and prevent missed claims.
kr.careers.challenge.1.description: US Medicare RPM/CCM billing requires deliverables in specific formats for each CPT code. We are developing an AI pipeline that analyzes patient vital data and care records to automatically generate reports meeting each code's requirements. The goal is to reduce clinician administrative burden and prevent missed claims.
careers.challenge.2.title: Multilingual Senior Usability Optimization
kr.careers.challenge.2.title: Multilingual Senior Usability Optimization
careers.challenge.2.description: The primary users of the patient app (Hicare App) are patients aged 65 and older. The app supports English, Spanish, and Korean with voice-guided measurement prompts and an intuitive interface. The challenge is designing UX that accounts for varying digital literacy levels among elderly users from diverse cultural backgrounds.

business.careportal.title: Hicare Care Portal
business.careportal.intro: A SaaS platform that automates RPM/CCM billing for US clinics.
business.careportal.problem.title: The Problem Clinics Face
business.careportal.problem.description: To bill for RPM/CCM, clinics must document detailed deliverables including patient care records, monitoring data, consultation notes, and management time logs. This administrative burden is significant for clinicians, and incomplete documentation leads to missed claims.
business.careportal.solution.title: How Care Portal Solves It
business.careportal.problem1.title: Patient Care Workflow
business.careportal.problem1.desc: Patient enrollment, vital data monitoring, abnormal value alerts, consultation logging, and care plan management are all handled within a single platform.
business.careportal.problem2.title: Automated Documentation
business.careportal.problem2.desc: Automatically generates the deliverables required for RPM/CCM billing, including management time, consultation records, and monitoring logs. Reduces clinician administrative burden and prevents missed claims.
business.careportal.problem3.title: Billing Integration
business.careportal.problem3.desc: Automatically generates reports aligned with RPM CPT codes (99453, 99454, 99457, 99458) and CCM CPT codes (99490, 99487, 99489), linking them to Medicare insurance claims.
business.careportal.flow.description: HicareNet (provides Care Portal) -> Clinic (manages patients, performs monitoring) -> Patient (transmits vital data) -> CMS (reimburses insurance claims)
business.careportal.flow.cms_note: CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) is the US public insurance administration agency. The process is: clinics manage patients, document via Care Portal, then submit claims to CMS.
business.careportal.flow.device_note: Medical devices are FDA-approved devices owned by the clinics themselves. HicareNet does not supply or integrate devices.
business.careportal.feat1: Clinician Dashboard: Patient list, abnormal value alerts, priority-based sorting
business.careportal.feat2: AI-Powered Report Generation: Automatically generates documentation meeting CPT code-specific requirements
business.careportal.feat3: Time Tracking & Documentation Automation: Automatically logs call records, care plans, and management time
business.careportal.feat4: Multilingual UI: English, Spanish, and Korean
business.careportal.feat5: Patient App (Hicare App): Senior-friendly interface with voice-guided measurement prompts
business.careportal.revenue.title: Revenue Model
business.careportal.revenue.description: A SaaS monthly subscription model. Clinics pay HicareNet a platform usage fee. Clinics use Care Portal to manage patients and bill Medicare for reimbursement. Revenue generation of approximately $50 per patient per month is possible (varies by condition and service type).
business.careportal.performance.claims: More than 300 insurance claims processed per month
business.careportal.performance.target: 2025 target: East Coast expansion, 100 additional clinic contracts ($6,000,000 goal)
about.title: About Us
about.hero.headline:
Two Decades of HealthTech,
Validated by US Market Trust
about.hero.subtext:
Built on deep healthcare domain expertise,
we're growing as a trusted technology partner for US Medicare clinics.
about.mission.text: In the US Medicare chronic care management market, we improve both clinic profitability and patient health outcomes through technology-driven billing automation. Our goal is to create an environment where clinicians can focus on patient care instead of administrative tasks.
kr.careers.hero.headline: Building US Healthcare SaaS<br class="hidden sm:block"> from Korea
faq.hero.headline: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.hero.subtext: Answers to common questions from investors, job applicants, and partners.
faq.investor.q1.question: Where does HicareNet generate its revenue?
faq.investor.q1.answer: Our primary revenue source is the monthly subscription fee from the Care Portal SaaS platform provided to US clinics. Clinics use Care Portal to manage RPM/CCM patients and submit Medicare claims, paying HicareNet a platform usage fee. As of 2024, overseas revenue accounts for 96.68% of total revenue, with the majority generated in the United States.
faq.investor.q2.question: Can the service be offered in countries other than the US?
faq.investor.q2.answer: Care Portal is a platform optimized for the US Medicare reimbursement system. Since the RPM/CCM insurance billing structure is unique to the US, Care Portal services are currently focused on the US market. Our European operations (Italy, France) involve the export of gateways and measurement devices. Expanding Care Portal internationally would require evaluation based on each country's healthcare insurance system.
faq.investor.q3.question: What differentiates HicareNet from competitors?
faq.investor.q3.answer: There are three key differentiators. First, the level of billing documentation automation: our AI-powered system automatically generates reports that meet CPT code-specific requirements, reducing clinician administrative burden and preventing missed claims. Second, multilingual and multicultural support: the platform supports English, Spanish, and Korean UI, enabling service delivery to diverse patient communities in the US. Third, a senior-friendly patient app: designed with voice guidance and an intuitive interface so that elderly patients can independently take measurements and transmit data.
faq.recruit.q1.question: Can I apply without US healthcare domain experience?
faq.recruit.q1.answer: Yes. During the onboarding process, we provide training on the US Medicare system, RPM/CCM billing structure, HIPAA regulations, and other domain knowledge required for the role. We primarily evaluate candidates based on their professional expertise in software development, planning, or business development.
faq.recruit.q2.question: What level of English proficiency is required?
faq.recruit.q2.answer: Basic English reading and writing skills are required. You will need to read English documents and exchange emails and messages as part of collaboration with the US subsidiary. Fluent spoken English is not mandatory, and the required level varies by role and position.
faq.recruit.q3.question: Is remote work available?
faq.recruit.q3.answer: Details on the remote work policy are currently being finalized. This page will be updated as soon as the policy is confirmed. For employment-related inquiries, please contact us through the Contact page.
faq.partner.q1.question: We are looking to supply domestic medical devices to the US market. Is collaboration possible?
faq.partner.q1.answer: HicareNet has over 20 years of technical expertise integrating more than 120 types of medical devices, along with experience exporting medical devices internationally, including through the US VA (Department of Veterans Affairs) project. For specific discussions about device integration and US market supply partnerships with domestic medical device companies, please contact our Business Development team via the Contact page.
faq.partner.q2.question: Can we collaborate on government R&D projects?
faq.partner.q2.answer: Yes. HicareNet has participated in government support programs such as the NIPA Americas ICT Business Partnership, and operates Corporate R&D Institute No. 1. If you are interested in joint R&D projects in the areas of digital healthcare, remote patient management, or AI-powered medical solutions, please reach out through our Contact page.
global.hero.headline:
Four Countries, Four Medical Fields,
One Technology Platform
global.hero.subtext:
Operating healthcare technology alongside local medical partners
in the US, Italy, France, and Thailand.
global.map.title: Global Operations
global.map.intro: Explore our business presence and key achievements in each country.
global.map.marker_us_portal: Care Portal SaaS service operations, contracts with 40 clinics
global.map.marker_italy: 1,000 gateways exported, cardiac patient discharge management
global.map.marker_thailand: Collaboration with healthcare institutions and pharmacy chains
global.portal.title: Care Portal Performance (United States)
global.portal.body: The service launched in April 2023 in the California area. Starting with 15 clinics and 1,000 patients by the end of 2023, it expanded to 37 clinics and over 2,000 patients in 2024. As of 2025, over 3,000 patients are enrolled across 40 clinics, with more than 300 Medicare claims processed monthly.
global.portal.expansion_body: We are expanding services to the US East Coast and South. The goal is to secure 100 additional clinics in regions with concentrated CMS budgets, such as Florida, New York, and Texas. The total contract target is $6,000,000 (based on 100 clinics).
global.export.title: Medical Device Export Track Record
global.export.va.title: US VA (Department of Veterans Affairs)
global.export.va.body: Under the US federal VA project, over 10,000 Hicare Hub gateways were exported for a remote home care solution for veterans. This project established the foundation for HicareNet's entry into the US market.
global.export.italy.title: Italy
global.export.italy.body: In partnership with CEGLIE MESSAPICA Hospital in the Puglia region of southern Italy, 1,000 gateways and measurement devices were exported. Post-discharge health management services are provided to over 300 cardiac surgery patients.
global.export.france.title: France
global.export.france.body: As part of a project to establish Small Healthcare Centers within a French pharmacy chain, 3,000 gateways and related measurement devices were exported. These are used for health management services for pharmacy visitors.
global.partnership.title: Partnerships
global.partnership.amc.title: AMC Health MoU (2025)
global.partnership.amc.body: A Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) was signed with AMC Health, a US telehealth company, establishing a partnership foundation for service expansion in the US market.
global.partnership.nipa.title: NIPA Americas ICT Business Partnership
global.partnership.nipa.body: HicareNet is participating in the NIPA (National IT Industry Promotion Agency) Americas ICT Business Partnership program to expand its local partner network in the United States.

ir.hero.subtext: Financial status, market opportunities, and business progress — shared with you regularly.
ir.overview.title: Company Overview
ir.overview.employees: 22 (R&D 10, BI 7, Management Support 4, Overseas Support Team separate)
ir.overview.business: Digital Healthcare (US Medicare-based RPM/CCM SaaS Platform)
ir.revenue.title: Revenue Trends (2022-2025)
ir.revenue.commentary_paragraph_1: The revenue decline in 2023 was due to a structural business transition. Revenue temporarily decreased as the company shifted its revenue base from the domestic market to the US market.
ir.revenue.commentary_paragraph_2: Starting in 2024, revenue from the US Care Portal service began to be fully reflected. The company recorded $998,404 in overseas revenue in 2024, and achieved $816,668 in just the first half of 2025, demonstrating rapid year-over-year growth.
ir.revenue.commentary_paragraph_3: The overseas revenue ratio dropped sharply from 95.93% in 2022 to 3.05% in 2023, then recovered to 96.68% in 2024. This demonstrates that the business structure has stabilized around the overseas (US) market.
ir.market.title: Market Opportunity
ir.market.rpm.title: US RPM Market
ir.market.rpm.body: The market is projected to grow from approximately $1.7B in 2023 to approximately $6.3B by 2030, at a CAGR of 18.4%.
ir.market.ccm.title: US CCM Market
ir.market.ccm.body: The market was valued at approximately $2.2B in 2024 and is growing at a CAGR of 11.7%.
ir.market.rpm_users.title: RPM User Population
ir.market.rpm_users.body: As of 2025, an estimated 71 million people in the US use RPM services. This figure includes a broad definition encompassing wearable/app-based monitoring; the number of clinical RPM users under Medicare CPT codes is smaller.
ir.market.apcm.title: APCM: New Market Opportunity
ir.market.apcm.body: CMS introduced APCM (Advanced Primary Care Management) as a new reimbursement framework effective January 2025. It consolidates previously fragmented chronic care management codes such as CCM, and expands eligibility to patients with one or more chronic conditions. Care Portal is preparing feature enhancements to support APCM codes (G0556, G0557).
ir.contact.title: IR Inquiries
ir.contact.body: For IR-related inquiries, please use the contact information below.
newsroom.title: Newsroom
newsroom.news1.title: Reached 40 US Clinic Contracts
newsroom.news1.body: As of H1 2025, the number of US clinics contracted for Care Portal services reached 40. Over 3,000 patients are enrolled, and more than 300 Medicare insurance claims are processed monthly. H1 revenue reached $816,668.
newsroom.news2.title: MoU Signed with AMC Health
newsroom.news2.body: A Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) was signed with AMC Health, a US telehealth company, establishing a partnership foundation for expansion in the US market.
newsroom.news3.title: Participation in NIPA Americas ICT Business Partnership
newsroom.news3.body: HicareNet is participating in the NIPA (National IT Industry Promotion Agency) Americas ICT Business Partnership program to expand its local network in the United States.
newsroom.news4.title: 2024 Overseas Revenue Reaches $998,404
newsroom.news4.body: Driven by the expansion of Care Portal services in the US, overseas revenue reached $998,404 in 2024. The service was used by 37 clinics and over 2,000 patients.
newsroom.news5.title: Medical Device Exports to Italy and France
newsroom.news5.body: 1,000 gateways were exported to CEGLIE MESSAPICA Hospital in Italy and 3,000 to a pharmacy chain in France. Cumulative device exports have exceeded 14,000 units.
newsroom.news6.title: US Subsidiary Established
newsroom.news6.body: A US subsidiary was established in California, creating a base for full-scale entry into the US Medicare market.
technology.hero.headline:
The Technical Foundation Behind
a Platform You Can Trust
technology.hero.subtext:
Cloud infrastructure, AI-powered medical data analysis, and 20 years of domain expertise.
The technology that enables accurate billing and reliable operations.
technology.architecture.title: Care Portal Technical Architecture
technology.architecture.body: Care Portal is a cloud-based SaaS platform. It handles RPM/CCM patient care workflows and Medicare billing processes for US clinics within a single system.
technology.architecture.layer_1_desc: Collects and transmits biometric data in real time from the patient app and connected devices.
technology.architecture.layer_2_desc: Analyzes collected data to detect anomalies and automatically generates reports that meet CPT code-specific requirements.
technology.architecture.layer_3_desc: Documents management time, consultation records, and monitoring logs to automatically produce the deliverables required for Medicare claims.
technology.architecture.platform_note: Cross-platform support: Android, iOS, Tablet, and Web
technology.architecture.lab_note: Operates Corporate R&D Institute No. 1; R&D staff accounts for 45% of total employees
technology.ai.title: AI-Powered Medical Data Analysis
technology.ai.report.title: AI Automated Report Generation
technology.ai.report.desc: Analyzes patient biometric data and care records to automatically generate reports that meet RPM/CCM CPT code-specific requirements. This reduces clinician administrative burden and prevents missed claims.
technology.ai.anomaly.title: Anomaly Prediction
technology.ai.anomaly.desc: Continuously learns patterns in patient biometric data to detect risk signals before abnormal values occur. Sends prioritized alerts to clinicians to enable proactive intervention.
technology.ai.pneumonia.title: AI-Assisted Pneumonia Diagnosis Medical Device
technology.ai.pneumonia.desc: An AI-powered medical device for pneumonia diagnosis support is under development, with FDA approval currently being pursued.
technology.ai.dementia.title: AI-Assisted Dementia Diagnosis Medical Device
technology.ai.dementia.desc: An AI-powered medical device for dementia diagnosis support is currently in the preparatory stage.
technology.security.title: Security and Certifications
technology.security.hipaa.title: HIPAA Compliance
technology.security.hipaa.desc: We comply with HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act). Security standards required by federal regulations are met throughout the entire process of collecting, storing, and transmitting patient health information.
technology.security.iso27001.title: ISO 27001 Certified
technology.security.iso27001.desc: We have obtained ISO 27001 certification, the international standard for information security management systems. We operate a systematic management framework to protect the organization's information assets.
technology.security.iso27701.title: ISO 27701 Certified
technology.security.iso27701.desc: We have obtained ISO 27701 certification, the international standard for privacy information management systems. We maintain a global-standard management framework for the collection and processing of personally identifiable information (PII).
technology.heritage.title: 20 Years of Accumulated Medical Domain Expertise
technology.heritage.body: HicareNet's technology is built on remote patient monitoring capabilities accumulated since the 2000s through InSung Information's Healthcare Division.
technology.heritage.devices_count: We have the technology to automatically pair with over 120 types of FDA-approved medical devices via Bluetooth. This includes blood pressure monitors, glucose meters, pulse oximeters, scales, and thermometers, all connected through auto-pairing. The system is designed so that even elderly patients can easily use it with voice-guided instructions.
technology.heritage.gateway_export: This technology was proven through the US VA project, with over 10,000 gateways exported, and has also been supplied to healthcare institutions in Italy and France.
technology.heritage.closing: This accumulated medical domain expertise is what enabled us to deeply understand the requirements of the US Medicare market and design Care Portal accordingly.

business.careportal.medicare.p1: Medicare is a US public insurance program for individuals aged 65 and older and certain people with disabilities. Medicare reimburses clinics for remote health management of patients with chronic conditions.
business.careportal.medicare.p2: RPM (Remote Patient Monitoring) is a service that remotely collects and analyzes patient vital data such as blood pressure and blood glucose. CCM (Chronic Care Management) is a non-face-to-face care management service for patients with chronic conditions. Clinics that provide these services can bill Medicare to generate revenue.
business.careportal.medicare.p3: Care Portal is a SaaS platform that automates the entire billing process for these services.
business.careportal.flow.title: Service Flow
business.careportal.features.title: Care Portal Key Features
business.careportal.feat1.title: Clinician Dashboard
business.careportal.feat1.desc: Patient list, abnormal value alerts, priority-based sorting
business.careportal.feat2.title: AI-Powered Report Generation
business.careportal.feat2.desc: Automatically generates documentation meeting CPT code-specific requirements
business.careportal.feat3.title: Time Tracking & Documentation Automation
business.careportal.feat3.desc: Automatically logs call records, care plans, and management time
business.careportal.feat4.title: Multilingual UI
business.careportal.feat5.title: Patient App (Hicare App)
business.careportal.feat5.desc: Senior-friendly interface with voice-guided measurement prompts
business.careportal.revenue.desc1: A SaaS monthly subscription model. Clinics pay HicareNet a platform usage fee. Clinics use Care Portal to manage patients and bill Medicare for reimbursement.
business.careportal.revenue.desc2: Revenue generation of approximately $50 per patient per month is possible (varies by condition and service type).
business.careportal.performance.title: Performance
business.expansion.apcm.desc: A new primary care-based chronic care management reimbursement program introduced by CMS in 2025. It extends the existing CCM model with CPT codes G0556, G0557, and G0558.
business.expansion.awv.desc: An annual health checkup program for Medicare beneficiaries. Requires completion of an HRA form and creation of a personalized prevention plan. Care Portal is preparing workflow automation for AWV.
business.expansion.hra.desc: A required component of AWV for health risk evaluation. We plan to develop form automation and results analysis features.
business.expansion.vbc.desc: The shift from fee-for-service to outcomes-based reimbursement. After accumulating RPM/CCM/APCM data, we plan to evolve into a platform that supports contracts based on patient health outcomes.
business.devices.export.title: Key Export Track Record
business.devices.export.va_desc: Remote home care solution for veterans. This US federal government project served as the foundation for US market entry.
business.devices.export.italy_desc: CEGLIE MESSAPICA Hospital. Cardiac patient discharge management platform. Serving over 300 patients in the Puglia region.
business.devices.export.france_desc: Health service centers within a pharmacy chain.
business.devices.growth_summary: Cumulative device exports: over 14,000 units. Starting from hardware and device integration technology, the business has since transitioned to a software platform (Care Portal) focus.

business.careportal.billing.section_subt: Care Portal automatically selects optimal codes based on patient data
business.careportal.billing.highlight: 2026 CMS Update: Revenue is generated even when patients measure only 2 days per month or management time is just 10 minutes
business.careportal.billing.code_99453: Initial device setup and patient education
business.careportal.revenue.desc2: Generate $73-$147 per patient per month in Medicare reimbursement (including 2026 CMS new codes, revenue even with partial compliance).
technology.ai.codeopt.title: Auto Code Optimization
technology.ai.codeopt.desc: Tracks each patient's data transmission days and management time in real-time to automatically select the optimal CPT code between 99445 vs 99454 (device) and 99470 vs 99457 (treatment management). Supports all billing codes including 2026 CMS new codes.
ir.market.rpm2026.title: RPM Code Expansion (CY2026)
ir.market.rpm2026.body: Starting January 2026, CMS introduced new RPM codes (99445, 99470). Patients who previously fell short of the 16-day/20-minute minimums are now billable, expanding the RPM patient pool by an estimated 30-40%. Care Portal automatically recognizes new codes and selects optimal billing codes.
faq.investor.q4.question: Are there RPM billing rule changes in 2026?
faq.investor.q4.answer: Yes. CMS CY2026 PFS Final Rule introduced CPT 99445 (2-15 day device monitoring, ~$47-52) and 99470 (10-minute treatment management, ~$26). Previously, failing to meet the 16-day/20-minute minimums meant zero revenue. Now partial compliance still generates reimbursement. Care Portal supports both new codes automatically.
faq.investor.q5.question: What is CPT code 99439 in CCM?
faq.investor.q5.answer: 99439 is an add-on code for basic CCM (99490) covering each additional 20 minutes, billable up to 2 times per month. This expands CCM revenue from $64/month to up to $164/month. Care Portal automatically tracks management time and determines 99439 billing eligibility.
